question: An error end event has its `errorCode` configured as the FEEL expression `=failureReason`, and an error event subprocess in the parent scope omits the error reference entirely. When the end event is reached with `failureReason = "PAYMENT_DECLINED"`, what happens?

options.text: The catch-all event subprocess catches the error regardless of the evaluated code.
An incident is raised because catch events cannot use expressions for `errorCode`.
The error is unhandled because the catch event has no `errorCode` defined.
The expression is rejected at deployment because throw events only accept static `errorCode` values.

a.text: Throw events may define `errorCode` as a FEEL expression, and an error catch event that omits the error reference catches all thrown errors.

b.text: The static-value restriction applies to the catch event's `errorCode`, not to the throw event; the catch event here has no `errorCode` at all because it is catch-all.

c.text: An error catch event that omits the error reference catches all thrown errors per the docs, so the error is handled.

d.text: Throw events explicitly accept expressions for `errorCode`; only catch events are restricted to static values.

explanation: On an error throw event the `errorCode` may be an expression evaluated when the event is reached. On a catch event the `errorCode` must be static, but the catch event may omit the error reference entirely, in which case it catches all thrown errors.
